news.title: 1 in 4 workers stay in unwanted jobs for health coverage, survey finds
news.summary: More Americans are keeping jobs they would otherwise leave for fear of losing their insurance, as healthcare costs rise and nonemployer coverage seems less affordable, according to a new poll.

news.title: Men use outside job offers to negotiate pay while women do not, per Swedish study
news.summary: Researchers recommended implementing structured wage-setting practices that reduce the role of bargaining in setting pay.

news.title: Federal HR office lost more than a third of its workforce during Trump’s second term, GAO reports
news.summary: The watchdog said the losses hampered “operational capacity” at the U.S. Office of Personnel Management and “risk worsening pre-existing skills gaps.”

news.title: The ‘average’ mind doesn’t exist: How work experts are thinking about neurodiversity
news.summary: HR may need to bridge the gap when it comes to their workforce’s empathy and understanding for neurodiverse workers.

news.title: AI skills gap persists despite widening personal use
news.summary: Companies assuming employees will bring AI skills into their jobs will set themselves up to fail, according to CompTIA Vice President of Research Seth Robinson.

news.title: New grads have to compete with AI for entry-level roles, hiring managers say
news.summary: Nearly half of organizations now ask a senior worker plus AI to do the work of several entry-level grads, per a new report.

news.title: Retirement benefits suffer from lack of engagement, not trust, NFP says
news.summary: The findings come as more U.S. workers are “off track” for retirement, according to the Aon-owned broker.

news.title: H-1B visa program hits 2027 cap
news.summary: While the second Trump administration has stepped up its efforts to discourage use of the program, many top employers continue to take advantage of it.

news.title: 3rd Circuit: Employers need not ‘go digging for disabilities’
news.summary: A midnight-shift employee fired for sleeping on the job mentioned fatigue and dry eye too late to properly invoke the Americans with Disabilities Act, the appeals court said.

news.title: Peak office use hits 80%, topping pre-pandemic levels, CBRE says
news.summary: Driving the gains are attendance policies and managers doing a better job matching space with what employees want, the company says.
